members.name: AddPolygon
members.summary: Adds a polygon using the indices of the vertices you get when calling [method get_vertices].

members.name: Clear
members.summary: Clears the internal arrays for vertices and polygon indices.

members.name: ClearPolygons
members.summary: Clears the array of polygons, but it doesn't clear the array of vertices.

members.name: CreateFromMesh
members.summary: Initializes the navigation mesh by setting the vertices and indices according to a [Mesh]. [b]Note:[/b] The given [param mesh] must be of type [constant Mesh.PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLES] and have an index array.

members.name: GetCollisionMaskValue
members.summary: Returns whether or not the specified layer of the [member geometry_collision_mask] is enabled, given a [param layer_number] between 1 and 32.

members.name: GetPolygon
members.summary: Returns a [PackedInt32Array] containing the indices of the vertices of a created polygon.

members.name: GetPolygonCount
members.summary: Returns the number of polygons in the navigation mesh.

members.name: GetVertices
members.summary: Returns a [PackedVector3Array] containing all the vertices being used to create the polygons.

members.name: SetCollisionMaskValue
members.signature: public void SetCollisionMaskValue(int layerNumber, bool value)
members.summary: Based on [param value], enables or disables the specified layer in the [member geometry_collision_mask], given a [param layer_number] between 1 and 32.

members.name: SetVertices
members.summary: Sets the vertices that can be then indexed to create polygons with the [method add_polygon] method.

members.name: AgentHeight
members.summary: The minimum floor to ceiling height that will still allow the floor area to be considered walkable. [b]Note:[/b] While baking, this value will be rounded up to the nearest multiple of [member cell_height].

members.name: AgentMaxClimb
members.summary: The minimum ledge height that is considered to still be traversable. [b]Note:[/b] While baking, this value will be rounded down to the nearest multiple of [member cell_height].

members.name: AgentMaxSlope
members.summary: The maximum slope that is considered walkable, in degrees.

members.name: AgentRadius
members.summary: The distance to erode/shrink the walkable area of the heightfield away from obstructions. [b]Note:[/b] While baking, this value will be rounded up to the nearest multiple of [member cell_size]. [b]Note:[/b] The radius must be equal or higher than [code]0.0[/code]. If the radius is [code]0.0[/code], it won't be possible to fix invalid outline overlaps and other precision errors during the baking process. As a result, some obstacles may be excluded incorrectly from the final navigation mesh, or may delete the navigation mesh's polygons.

members.name: BorderSize
members.summary: The size of the non-navigable border around the bake bounding area. In conjunction with the [member filter_baking_aabb] and a [member edge_max_error] value at [code]1.0[/code] or below the border size can be used to bake tile aligned navigation meshes without the tile edges being shrunk by [member agent_radius]. [b]Note:[/b] If this value is not [code]0.0[/code], it will be rounded up to the nearest multiple of [member cell_size] during baking.

members.name: CellHeight
members.summary: The cell height used to rasterize the navigation mesh vertices on the Y axis. Must match with the cell height on the navigation map.

members.name: CellSize
members.summary: The cell size used to rasterize the navigation mesh vertices on the XZ plane. Must match with the cell size on the navigation map.

members.name: DetailSampleDistance
members.summary: The sampling distance to use when generating the detail mesh, in cell unit.

members.name: DetailSampleMaxError
members.summary: The maximum distance the detail mesh surface should deviate from heightfield, in cell unit.

members.name: EdgeMaxError
members.summary: The maximum distance a simplified contour's border edges should deviate the original raw contour.

members.name: EdgeMaxLength
members.summary: The maximum allowed length for contour edges along the border of the mesh. A value of [code]0.0[/code] disables this feature. [b]Note:[/b] While baking, this value will be rounded up to the nearest multiple of [member cell_size].

members.name: FilterBakingAabb
members.summary: If the baking [AABB] has a volume the navigation mesh baking will be restricted to its enclosing area.

members.name: FilterBakingAabbOffset
members.summary: The position offset applied to the [member filter_baking_aabb] [AABB].

members.name: FilterLedgeSpans
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], marks spans that are ledges as non-walkable.

members.name: FilterLowHangingObstacles
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], marks non-walkable spans as walkable if their maximum is within [member agent_max_climb] of a walkable neighbor.

members.name: FilterWalkableLowHeightSpans
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], marks walkable spans as not walkable if the clearance above the span is less than [member agent_height].

members.name: GeometryCollisionMask
members.summary: The physics layers to scan for static colliders. Only used when [member geometry_parsed_geometry_type] is [constant PARSED_GEOMETRY_STATIC_COLLIDERS] or [constant PARSED_GEOMETRY_BOTH].

members.name: GeometryParsedGeometryType
members.summary: Determines which type of nodes will be parsed as geometry.

members.name: GeometrySourceGeometryMode
members.summary: The source of the geometry used when baking.

members.name: GeometrySourceGroupName
members.summary: The name of the group to scan for geometry. Only used when [member geometry_source_geometry_mode] is [constant SOURCE_GEOMETRY_GROUPS_WITH_CHILDREN] or [constant SOURCE_GEOMETRY_GROUPS_EXPLICIT].

members.name: RegionMergeSize
members.summary: Any regions with a size smaller than this will be merged with larger regions if possible. [b]Note:[/b] This value will be squared to calculate the number of cells. For example, a value of 20 will set the number of cells to 400.

members.name: RegionMinSize
members.summary: The minimum size of a region for it to be created. [b]Note:[/b] This value will be squared to calculate the minimum number of cells allowed to form isolated island areas. For example, a value of 8 will set the number of cells to 64.

members.name: SamplePartitionType
members.summary: Partitioning algorithm for creating the navigation mesh polys.

members.name: VerticesPerPolygon
members.summary: The maximum number of vertices allowed for polygons generated during the contour to polygon conversion process.

values.name: PARSED_GEOMETRY_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ParsedGeometryType] enum.

values.name: PARSED_GEOMETRY_MESH_INSTANCES
values.summary: Parses mesh instances as geometry. This includes [MeshInstance3D], [CSGShape3D], and [GridMap] nodes.

values.name: PARSED_GEOMETRY_STATIC_COLLIDERS
values.summary: Parses [StaticBody3D] colliders as geometry. The collider should be in any of the layers specified by [member geometry_collision_mask].

values.name: SAMPLE_PARTITION_LAYERS
values.summary: Layer partitioning. Good choice to use for tiled navigation mesh with medium and small sized tiles.

values.name: SAMPLE_PARTITION_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum SamplePartitionType] enum.

values.name: SAMPLE_PARTITION_MONOTONE
values.summary: Monotone partitioning. Use this if you want fast navigation mesh generation.

values.name: SAMPLE_PARTITION_WATERSHED
values.summary: Watershed partitioning. Generally the best choice if you precompute the navigation mesh, use this if you have large open areas.

values.name: SOURCE_GEOMETRY_GROUPS_EXPLICIT
values.summary: Uses nodes in a group for geometry. The group is specified by [member geometry_source_group_name].

values.name: SOURCE_GEOMETRY_GROUPS_WITH_CHILDREN
values.summary: Scans nodes in a group and their child nodes recursively for geometry. The group is specified by [member geometry_source_group_name].

values.name: SOURCE_GEOMETRY_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum SourceGeometryMode] enum.

values.name: SOURCE_GEOMETRY_ROOT_NODE_CHILDREN
values.summary: Scans the child nodes of the root node recursively for geometry.

constants.name: SAMPLE_PARTITION_WATERSHED
constants.summary: Watershed partitioning. Generally the best choice if you precompute the navigation mesh, use this if you have large open areas.

constants.name: SAMPLE_PARTITION_MONOTONE
constants.summary: Monotone partitioning. Use this if you want fast navigation mesh generation.

constants.name: SAMPLE_PARTITION_LAYERS
constants.summary: Layer partitioning. Good choice to use for tiled navigation mesh with medium and small sized tiles.

constants.name: SAMPLE_PARTITION_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum SamplePartitionType] enum.

constants.name: PARSED_GEOMETRY_MESH_INSTANCES
constants.summary: Parses mesh instances as geometry. This includes [MeshInstance3D], [CSGShape3D], and [GridMap] nodes.

constants.name: PARSED_GEOMETRY_STATIC_COLLIDERS
constants.summary: Parses [StaticBody3D] colliders as geometry. The collider should be in any of the layers specified by [member geometry_collision_mask].

constants.name: PARSED_GEOMETRY_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ParsedGeometryType] enum.

constants.name: SOURCE_GEOMETRY_ROOT_NODE_CHILDREN
constants.summary: Scans the child nodes of the root node recursively for geometry.

constants.name: SOURCE_GEOMETRY_GROUPS_WITH_CHILDREN
constants.summary: Scans nodes in a group and their child nodes recursively for geometry. The group is specified by [member geometry_source_group_name].

constants.name: SOURCE_GEOMETRY_GROUPS_EXPLICIT
constants.summary: Uses nodes in a group for geometry. The group is specified by [member geometry_source_group_name].

constants.name: SOURCE_GEOMETRY_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum SourceGeometryMode] enum.
